template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — PERSONAL INJURY INTAKE QUESTIONNAIRE**:
This is a STRUCTURED BLANK FORM the client fills out (paper or digital) OR a guided interview script the intake attorney/paralegal follows. Generate the FORM, not filled-in answers. Target length: 3–5 printed pages depending on which optional sections are enabled. Use markdown with `##` for major sections and `###` for sub-sections. Use `[ ]` for checkboxes, `____________` (underscores, ~30 chars) for short answer lines, `______________________________________________` for long-answer lines, and standalone paragraph blocks with 5–8 blank lines for narrative answers.

DO NOT invent or pre-fill client data. All answer fields must be blank. The only data that MAY appear filled in is the firm's own contact/letterhead info at the top (pulled from firm data in the prompt context).

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `subPractice`: `mva` (default) | `premises` | `med_mal` | `workers_comp_third_party` | `product_liability` — selects which Section B (Incident Details) variant to emit. Emit exactly ONE variant.
- `formType`: `paper` (default) | `digital` | `interview_guide` — controls format:
  - `paper` → printable blank form, checkboxes and lined answer fields as described above.
  - `digital` → same structure but each field line is followed by a parenthetical machine-readable field name in lowercase_snake (e.g., `Full legal name: ____________ (client_full_name)`). Useful for form-builder import.
  - `interview_guide` → same questions reformatted as a script with attorney/paralegal prompts (`ASK:`) and note-taking prompts (`NOTE:`). No answer lines; bullet-point notes sections instead.
- `includeMedicalAuthSection`: boolean (default true) — when true, emit a bundled HIPAA-style authorization block at the end (separate from the main questionnaire). When false, suppress that block entirely.
- `includeWageLossSection`: boolean (default true) — when false, suppress Section E entirely.
- `language`: `en` (default) | `es` — when `es`, emit the entire form in Spanish. Do not mix languages. If `es`, replace the defined section titles with their Spanish equivalents and translate all questions directly — keep medical and legal terms (CPT, HIPAA) in English with a parenthetical Spanish gloss only on first use.

**STRUCTURE** (section letters ALWAYS in this order; suppressed sections are skipped without renumbering):

### Top of document
- Title line (bold): `PERSONAL INJURY INTAKE QUESTIONNAIRE` (or Spanish equivalent).
- One-line firm identifier under the title (firm name + phone + email from context).
- Instructions paragraph (2–3 sentences): explain that the form is confidential, used solely to evaluate representation, and asks the client to answer as completely as possible and leave blank anything they don't know.
- Date line and two client-signature lines at the very bottom (not the top).

### A. Client Information (always)
Questions for: full legal name; preferred name; date of birth; Social Security Number (last 4 only — explicitly say 'last 4 digits only'); current home address; mailing address (if different); cell phone; home phone; work phone; personal email; preferred contact method checkboxes; best time to reach; primary language; need interpreter (y/n); marital status; spouse name and contact (if applicable); minor children (names + DOBs); emergency contact name / relationship / phone.

### B. Incident Details (emit ONE variant based on `subPractice`)
- **mva**: date/time of accident; location (street, city, state, highway); weather/road conditions; client's role (driver / passenger / pedestrian / bicyclist / motorcyclist / other); direction of travel; direction of other vehicle; point of impact; vehicle make/model/year (client); vehicle make/model/year (other); airbag deployment y/n; seatbelt use y/n; police on scene (agency + report number); ambulance on scene y/n; ER transport y/n; description of how the accident happened (long-answer narrative block).
- **premises**: date/time of incident; exact location (premises name, address, area within premises); owner/occupant identity if known; nature of dangerous condition (wet floor / broken stair / inadequate lighting / unmarked hazard / other); how client first noticed the condition; how long condition existed (if known); whether any employees or agents were nearby before the incident; incident report filed with the premises y/n (and with whom); description of fall/incident mechanism (long-answer narrative block).
- **med_mal**: provider name and specialty; facility name and address; date of alleged malpractice; nature of medical procedure or treatment involved; who recommended the procedure/treatment; informed-consent process recollection; what the client believes went wrong; when the client first suspected negligence (for discovery-rule purposes); subsequent treating providers who opined about care; description of alleged malpractice (long-answer narrative block).
- **workers_comp_third_party**: date/time of injury; employer name and address; worksite address; job title and typical duties; tools/equipment/vehicle involved; third party identity if known (contractor, property owner, equipment manufacturer, driver); Workers' Compensation claim filed y/n, claim number, insurer; treating physician under WC; description of incident (long-answer narrative block).
- **product_liability**: product name, brand, model number, serial number; date/place of purchase; receipt/documentation kept y/n; date/time of injury; how the product was being used at the time; alleged defect category (design / manufacturing / warning / instruction); prior similar incidents with the product known to client y/n; product currently preserved y/n and where stored; description of failure mechanism (long-answer narrative block).

### C. Injuries and Body Parts (always)
Grid / checkbox list of body regions: head; neck / cervical spine; mid-back / thoracic spine; lower back / lumbar spine; right shoulder; left shoulder; right arm / elbow / wrist / hand; left arm / elbow / wrist / hand; chest / ribs; abdomen / pelvis / groin; right hip / leg / knee / ankle / foot; left hip / leg / knee / ankle / foot; internal injuries; psychological / PTSD / anxiety / depression. Under each checked region, ask for: nature of symptoms (pain, numbness, weakness, stiffness, loss of motion, scarring); onset (immediate / delayed — include delay duration); current severity 0–10; any ongoing treatment for that region. Include a long-answer block for any injury not fitting the grid.

### D. Treatment Providers (always)
Table or list format (one entry per provider). For each: provider name; specialty (e.g., ER, primary care, orthopedic, neurology, PT, chiropractic, pain management, psychiatric, surgical); facility name and address; phone; date of first visit; date of last visit; still treating y/n; estimated total bills for this provider. Leave space for 6 provider entries with a note that additional providers can be listed on an attached sheet. Include a prompt to bring EOBs, receipts, and prescription records to the next meeting.

### E. Employment and Wage Loss [suppress entirely if `includeWageLossSection=false`]
Employer name and address; job title; supervisor name and phone; employment status (full-time / part-time / contract / self-employed / unemployed / student / retired); hourly wage or salary (with unit: per hour / per week / per year); average hours per week; date client last worked before the incident; date client returned to work (or 'still out'); total days missed; modified or light duty y/n; lost bonuses/commissions/tips y/n; use of paid time off / sick leave / short-term disability / long-term disability (check all that apply); impact on promotions/career trajectory (narrative block). If self-employed: describe the business, average monthly revenue and expenses, how injury affected operations (narrative block).

### F. Insurance (always)
Two parallel blocks:
- **Client's own insurance**: health insurance carrier + policy/member number; auto insurer + policy number (applicable for MVA); homeowners/renters insurer + policy number (applicable for premises or pet bite); any MedPay / PIP / UM / UIM coverage on auto policy; disability insurance (short-term / long-term); umbrella policy y/n.
- **At-fault / third-party insurance known to client**: carrier name; policy number or claim number; adjuster name and phone; any written communication received from adjuster y/n (attach copies).

### G. Prior Claims and Pre-existing Conditions (always)
Prior personal injury claims / lawsuits y/n — if yes, date, nature, carrier, outcome (settled / dismissed / tried / still open); prior workers' compensation claims y/n — same details; pre-existing conditions affecting the currently injured body parts (e.g., prior neck injury if now claiming neck injury); prior surgeries on currently injured body parts; prior auto accidents in past 10 years; Social Security Disability or SSI history; any current pending claims.

### H. Witnesses (always)
List up to 5 witnesses. For each: name; relationship to client (friend, stranger, co-worker, passenger, bystander); address; phone; email; brief description of what they observed; whether they provided a statement at the scene and to whom.

### I. Evidence and Documentation Available (always)
Checklist with space for file/exhibit numbers or descriptions: photos of scene; photos of injuries; photos of vehicle/product damage; video (dashcam, security, body cam, cellphone); police or incident report (agency + report number); ambulance run sheet; ER discharge summary; medical bills; pharmacy receipts; pay stubs; tax returns; correspondence from insurance companies; text messages / social media messages related to the incident; any witness statements already obtained.

### J. Authorization to Share Information and Client Acknowledgments (always)
Statement that the client authorizes the firm to contact, on a preliminary basis for case evaluation only, any provider, employer, or insurer listed above. Acknowledgment that completing this form does NOT create an attorney-client relationship until a signed fee agreement is executed. Acknowledgment that all information provided is true and complete to the best of the client's knowledge. Two signature lines (Client / Date) and (Firm Representative / Date).

### Bundled Medical Authorization Block [suppress if `includeMedicalAuthSection=false`]
If enabled, append a short HIPAA-compliant release (NOT the full formal HIPAA authorization — that is a separate document) authorizing the firm to request, on a PRELIMINARY basis, treatment records and bills from the providers listed in Section D. Include: patient name; DOB; list of providers (referenced by name or 'see Section D of the attached intake questionnaire'); scope (records of treatment causally related to the incident dated [incident date]); expiration (6 months from signature or completion of representation evaluation, whichever first); signature and date. Include a single-line note: `See separate formal HIPAA Authorization (hipaa_authorization_pi) for records requests served on providers after representation begins.`

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Do NOT emit any section labeled with a suppressed-section letter (the remaining sections do NOT renumber — Sections A, B, C, D, F, G, H, I, J always appear; only E and the bundled auth block are conditional).
- Do NOT ask for the client's full SSN — last 4 digits only.
- Do NOT include any legal conclusions, causation opinions, damage valuations, or case-strategy language — this is a fact-gathering form only.
- Do NOT include jurisdiction-specific statute citations in the form text. The form is designed to be usable in any state; statutes attach at the fee-agreement / engagement-letter / HIPAA-authorization stage.
- Leave the firm letterhead / signature block areas for stationery injection — do NOT emit `Very truly yours,` or an attorney signature block (this is a form, not a letter).
- If `formType=interview_guide`, replace all answer lines with empty note-taking bullets under an `ASK:` prompt, and add a `NOTE:` prompt on sensitive questions (prior claims, pre-existing conditions) reminding the interviewer to probe gently.
hints: Personal-injury intake questionnaire. Generates a BLANK structured form for client completion (paper or digital) or an interview script (interview_guide). Sub-practice variant controls Section B (MVA / premises / med_mal / WC third-party / product liability). Sections A, C, D, F, G, H, I, J are always present. E (wage loss) and the bundled HIPAA-style preliminary auth block are conditional. Never pre-fill client answers; never include statute citations (this is pre-representation fact gathering, not a legal instrument). Supports English and Spanish output.
knownLimitations: Does not substitute for in-person intake interview — designed to supplement, not replace, attorney screening.
Pre-existing-condition and prior-claim questions are blunt; sensitive history often requires follow-up with the client.
Spanish translation (when `language=es`) should be reviewed by a bilingual attorney before use with clients — machine-generated translations of medical and legal terminology can drift.
Does not collect immigration status, sexual-orientation, or other sensitive attributes not needed for case evaluation — firms should not add such questions without a clear evidentiary purpose.
Bundled preliminary auth block is NOT a substitute for the formal HIPAA authorization used for records requests after representation begins — use `hipaa_authorization_pi` for that.
